diagrams.title: Centralized vs Decentralized vs Distributed Network Architecture
diagrams.description: Three network architecture comparison showing how data flows in different systems
elements: Centralized: Single central server node with multiple client nodes connected in star pattern, all data flows through center
Decentralized: Multiple server nodes connected in mesh, no single point of control, data flows between peer nodes
Distributed: Multiple identical nodes, each containing complete copy of data, decisions made collectively
Arrows showing data flow direction: solid arrows for requests, dashed for responses
Node icons: circles for central server, squares for peer nodes, triangles for distributed nodes
diagrams.purpose: Students understand the fundamental difference between centralized, decentralized, and distributed systems - critical for exam questions on blockchain advantages

diagrams.title: Blockchain Decentralization Layers Architecture
diagrams.description: Layered architecture showing how decentralization is achieved at different levels in blockchain
elements: Layer 1 - Network Layer: P2P network of nodes, each node equal, mesh topology
Layer 2 - Consensus Layer: Multiple consensus algorithms (PoW, PoS, DPoS), nodes participate in validation
Layer 3 - Data Layer: Distributed ledger, each node has copy of blockchain, merkle tree structure
Layer 4 - Application Layer: DApps, smart contracts, decentralized storage
Layer 5 - Governance Layer: DAO structures, on-chain voting, token-based voting rights
Vertical arrows showing how each layer contributes to decentralization
Labels showing protocols and technologies at each layer
diagrams.purpose: Students learn how blockchain achieves decentralization through multiple architectural layers - frequently asked in university exams

diagrams.title: Peer-to-Peer Network Topology in Blockchain
diagrams.description: Mesh network diagram showing equal peer nodes in blockchain P2P network
elements: 12-16 peer nodes arranged in circular mesh pattern
Each node connected to 3-4 neighboring nodes (degree of connectivity)
No central server or hierarchy visible
Node icons: uniform circles with node ID labels (Node 1, Node 2, etc.)
Network edges: bidirectional arrows between connected nodes
Red dashed circle showing full network boundary
Legend: Node = Validator/Full Node, Arrow = P2P Communication
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how P2P networks enable decentralization - nodes are equal participants with no central authority

diagrams.title: Smart Contract Execution Flow in Decentralized Environment
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing how smart contracts execute across decentralized network
elements: Start: User initiates transaction via DApp
Transaction sent to P2P network (cloud icon with multiple nodes)
Transaction pool (mempool) shown with pending transactions
Mining/Validation nodes: 3-4 validator nodes in parallel
Consensus: Nodes reach agreement (checkmark icons)
Smart Contract deployed on blockchain (rectangle with code icon)
Execution triggered by conditions (if-this-then-that diagram)
State change recorded on distributed ledger
All nodes update their copy of ledger synchronously
End: Transaction confirmed, output returned to user
diagrams.purpose: Students understand end-to-end execution of decentralized applications and smart contracts

diagrams.title: Methods of Decentralization Classification
diagrams.description: Classification diagram showing three main methods to achieve decentralization
elements: Three main branches from central concept:
1. Architectural Decentralization: Hardware distribution, geographic distribution, node diversity
2. Political Decentralization: Distributed governance, multi-stakeholder control, transparent rules
3. Logical Decentralization: Smart contracts, on-chain governance, token-based voting
Each branch splits into 2-3 sub-methods with examples
Icons representing each type: server icon for architectural, vote icon for political, contract icon for logical
Color coding: Blue for architectural, Green for political, Orange for logical
diagrams.purpose: Comprehensive understanding of how decentralization can be achieved - important for exam questions on decentralization methods

charts.title: Centralized vs Decentralized vs Distributed Systems Comparison
charts.content: Table with columns: Parameter | Centralized | Decentralized | Distributed; Rows: Control | Single authority | Multiple authorities | Shared control; Data Storage | One location | Few locations | Every node; Failure Point | Single point of failure | Few failure points | No single failure; Decision Making | Central node decides | Consensus among hubs | Collective decision; Scalability | Limited | Moderate | High; Examples | Traditional banks | Blockchain consortia | Bitcoin/Ethereum

charts.title: Methods of Decentralization - Detailed Classification
charts.content: Architectural Decentralization: Horizontal distribution (sharding), Geographic distribution (global nodes), Hardware diversity (different miner types); Political Decentralization: DAO governance, Token-weighted voting, Multi-signature control; Logical Decentralization: Smart contracts (self-executing), On-chain governance (voting), Merkle tree structure (verifiable)

charts.title: Decentralization Implementation Process
charts.content: Decision to Decentralize → Identify Central Points → Choose Decentralization Method → Select Consensus Mechanism → Design Governance Model → Implement Smart Contracts → Deploy Network → Monitor and Evolve; Each step shows actions and decisions required

charts.title: Traditional Organization vs DAO vs DAC vs DAS Comparison
charts.content: Table: Aspect | Traditional Org | DAO | DAC | DAS; Governance | Central leadership | Token-holder voting | Code + shareholders | Community consensus; Decision Speed | Fast | Medium (voting) | Fast (automated) | Slow (consensus); Legal Status | Recognized | Unclear | Emerging | Conceptual; Flexibility | High | Code-dependent | Market-driven | Community-driven; Examples | Companies | MakerDAO | The DAO | Concept only

branches: Core Concepts: Distributed ledger, P2P network, Consensus mechanisms, Cryptographic security
Methods of Decentralization: Architectural (hardware), Political (governance), Logical (smart contracts)
Ecosystem Components: Nodes, Miners/Validators, Full nodes, Light nodes, DApps, Smart contracts
Governance Models: DAO, DAC, DAS, Traditional centralized
Benefits: Trustlessness, Transparency, Immutability, Censorship resistance, Resilience
Platforms: Ethereum, Hyperledger, EOS, Tron, Polkadot
Challenges: Scalability, Regulation, User adoption, Energy consumption, Interoperability
Applications: DeFi, NFTs, Supply chain, Identity management, Voting systems
mindMaps.purpose: Comprehensive overview connecting all aspects of blockchain decentralization - useful for exam revision and understanding topic relationships

branches: Network Level: P2P communication, Node diversity, Geographic distribution
Data Level: Distributed ledger, Replication, Consensus on state
Application Level: DApps, Smart contracts, Decentralized storage
Economic Level: Token economics, Incentive mechanisms, Market-based coordination
Governance Level: On-chain voting, DAO structures, Protocol upgrades
Financial Level: DeFi protocols, Stablecoins, Decentralized exchanges
Social Level: Community-driven development, Open-source philosophy, User ownership
mindMaps.purpose: Shows complete ecosystem of blockchain decentralization from network to social level - helps students understand real-world implementation scope
